title: Public WiFi Safety
drDigitalIntro: Public WiFi at cafes, airports, and hotels is convenient, but less secure than your home network. Anyone on the same network could potentially intercept your data. Safety tips: avoid logging into banking or email on public WiFi, use HTTPS websites (the padlock), consider a VPN (Virtual Private Network) for an extra layer of security. Public WiFi is fine for reading news — just avoid anything sensitive.

playgroundTask.goal: You identified risks on public WiFi

steps.say: Go to the Phishing tab to check a suspicious WiFi login page.
Inspect 'Free WiFi Login' — this link is from a suspicious sign-in portal.
The URL is unsecured. Mark it Dangerous.
Now go to Privacy and turn off Location when on public networks.

drDigitalSuccess: You identified public WiFi risks! Be cautious about what you access on public networks.
drDigitalHint: Inspect links for unsecured URLs and limit what personal info your device shares on public networks.
